articles.title: Walmart-owned Flipkart, Amazon are squeezing India’s quick commerce startups
articles.description: Flipkart's ongoing expansion beyond major cities and heavy discounting is raising risks for India's quick commerce startups, analysts say.

articles.title: Kalshi wins temporary pause in Arizona criminal case
articles.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission announced Friday that it has won a temporary restraining order preventing Arizona from pursuing its criminal case against Kalshi.

articles.title: AMC will stream ‘The Audacity’ premiere in 21 parts on TikTok
articles.description: Is this a smart way to build buzz, or just an odd attempt to recreate Quibi?

articles.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
articles.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

articles.title: Nvidia-backed SiFive hits $3.65 billion valuation for open AI chips
articles.description: The deal is interesting for a number of reasons, including that SiFive's chip designs are based on RISC-V, not x86 or ARM.

articles.title: Podman avec des Quadlets + GitOps avec Materia
articles.description: Comment migrer ses docker-compose vers des quadlets Podman gérés par systemd, en passant par podlet pour la conversion et Materia pour le GitOps.

articles.title: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in ‘perfect’ landing for Moon mission
articles.description: The Integrity craft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego just after 5:07 p.m. Pacific Time.

articles.title: How to watch NASA’s Artemis II splash back down to Earth
articles.description: NASA's Artemis II mission has traveled farther from Earth than any mission before. Here's how to watch the crew's return landing in the Pacific Ocean.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

articles.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
articles.description: Ascend Elements said that it will be filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of a canceled government grant and a challenging market for lithium-ion batteries.

articles.title: Artemis II : objectif nul
articles.description: Merci au journal La Croix de m’avoir invité à rédiger cette tribune « opinion » sur le retour vers la Lune, dans le cadre de la mission Artémis II. Quatre milliards de dollars et des poussières : voilà ce qu’aura Artemis II : objectif nul first appeared on Mais où va le Web.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. articles.title: France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
articles.description: France's move to ditch Windows for Linux is its latest effort to reduce its reliance on American tech giants.

articles.title: YouTube Premium and YouTube Music are getting more expensive
articles.description: The YouTube Premium individual plan is increasing from $13.99 to $15.99 per month, while the family plan is increasing from $22.99 to $26.99 per month.

articles.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
articles.description: Fusion startups have raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of it going to a handful of companies.

articles.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
articles.description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

articles.title: PSA: If you use the Meta AI app, your friends will find out and it will be embarrassing
articles.description: Now that Meta released its Muse Spark model, more people are downloading the Meta AI app. And if you join the app, your friends will probably get an Instagram notification about it.

articles.title: Snap gets closer to releasing new AI glasses after years-long hiatus
articles.description: Specs has long been teasing the next version of its augmented reality glasses. A new partnership with Qualcomm promises some movement in that department.

articles.title: Automatisation de la palettisation : un marché en pleine explosion d’ici 2030
articles.description: Une transformation stratégique de la fin de ligne Longtemps considérée comme une simple étape logistique, la palettisation est aujourd’hui au cœur des stratégies d’automatisation industrielle. Dans un contexte marqué par la hausse des coûts de main-d’œuvre, les tensions sur le recrutement et la…

articles.title: ONNX : Le format universel pour déployer vos modèles ML
articles.description: ONNX : le format open source pour déployer vos modèles ML en production. Interopérabilité, performance, limites et cas d'usage.

articles.title: OpenAI dévoile ChatGPT Pro, une formule à 103 € par mois pour les développeurs
articles.description: Ce nouvel abonnement ChatGPT relève significativement les limites d'utilisation de Codex, l'agent de développement d'OpenAI.

articles.title: Claude Cowork disponible pour tous : Anthropic passe à l’échelle entreprise

articles.title:
[Test] GL.iNet Comet PoE (GL-RM1PE)
Le KVM IP qui m'a sauvé d'une catastrophe
articles.description: Le GL.iNet Comet PoE, c'est l'accès BIOS, clavier et écran à distance sur n'importe quelle machine sans IPMI. Test et retour d'expérience après quelques semaines.

articles.description: Une étude Yext portant sur 17,2 millions de citations montre que chaque modèle d'IA privilégie des types de sources distincts, remettant en question l'idée d'une stratégie uniforme.

articles.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI, alleging possible connection to FSU shooting
articles.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier plans to investigate OpenAI for its alleged harm to minors, potential to threaten national security, and its possible link to a shooting at Florida State University last year.

articles.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
articles.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

articles.title: EFF is the latest organization to leave X
articles.description: The EFF follows the departure of various news organizations and others who no longer find X a viable source of traffic.

articles.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna’s layoffs and recovery
articles.description: In 2021, Anjuna Security was growing fast, hiring aggressively, and chasing a market that seemed limitless. articles.title: Volkswagen drops all-electric ID.4 in the US in pivot back to gas SUVs
articles.description: All resources at its U.S. factory are aimed at the upcoming Atlas SUV.

articles.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
articles.description: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the incident.

articles.title: StubHub to pay $10M to settle FTC allegations over ‘deceptive’ ticket pricing
articles.description: The consumer protection agency says the company violated the FTC Act and its Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees by "deceptively" advertising ticket prices on its website without clearly disclosing upfront what the total cost would be, including all mandatory fees.

articles.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
articles.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

articles.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
articles.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

articles.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
articles.description: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?

articles.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
articles.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

articles.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
articles.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

articles.title: You can now edit your Instagram comments
articles.description: Instagram users can now edit comments, which is a long-awaited fix for anyone who’s ever had to delete and re-post a comment just to fix a typo.

articles.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
articles.description: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. Users simply describe what they need, prompting Ghostwriter to autonomously create…

articles.title: Avec’s Tinder-style email app allows you to swipe through your inbox

articles.description: X Chat brings back Voice Notes to DMs and group chats after removing the option during the upgrade.

articles.title: Hacker stole £700,000 from UK energy company by redirecting payment
articles.description: The U.K. energy company said a redirected payment meant for a contractor instead landed in a hacker's bank account.

articles.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
articles.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

articles.title: Sur Instagram, un paramètre « 13+ » filtre désormais les contenus inappropriés aux mineurs

articles.description: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. articles.title: Spotify now lets everyone turn off videos in its app

articles.title: Avec Muse Spark, Meta veut enfin rattraper OpenAI et Anthropic
articles.description: Distancé sur l'IA en dépit d'investissements massifs, le groupe californien tente de combler son retard avec Muse Spark. Un modèle qui sera prochainement déployé sur WhatsApp, Instagram et Facebook, en plus de l'application Meta AI.

articles.title: OpenAI modifie la tarification de Codex et passe à la facturation au token
articles.description: OpenAI modifie en profondeur la tarification de Codex. L'ancien système au message cède la place à une facturation indexée sur les tokens consommés. Le changement touche d'abord les clients Business et Enterprise.

articles.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account

articles.title: A self-driving car in Texas hit and killed a mother duck, sparking neighborhood outrage

articles.description: AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

articles.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindle devices
articles.description: Amazon told Kindle owners this week that it's ending support for all e-readers released in 2012 or earlier, making them virtually unable to load any new content.

articles.title: Meta debuts the Muse Spark model in a ‘ground-up overhaul’ of its AI

articles.description: Volkswagen subsidiary MOIA America still has a long and winding regulatory road before it can launch a commercial robotaxi service.

articles.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children

articles.title: Hackers steal and leak sensitive LAPD police documents
articles.description: The LAPD said the breach affected “a digital storage system” belonging to the city’s Attorney's Office. The World Leaks extortion gang was reported to be behind the attack.

articles.description: OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

articles.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
articles.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

articles.title: Comment YouTube s’est retrouvé noyé sous les publicités
articles.description: En deux ans, YouTube a neutralisé les adblockers, conquis les annonceurs et battu tous ses records. Ses 2,5 milliards d'utilisateurs, eux, ont surtout appris à compter jusqu'à quinze et à cliquer sur "Ignorer".

articles.description: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

articles.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups
articles.description: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

articles.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn
articles.description: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

articles.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
articles.description: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

articles.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says
articles.description: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test phase.

articles.title: Russian government hackers broke into thousands of home routers to steal passwords

articles.title: Hate jump scares? This app warns you before they happen

articles.title: Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon’s AI chips

articles.description: Anthropic bulked up its compute deal with Google and Broadcom as the company has seen its run-rate revenue surge to $30 billion.

articles.title: Trump administration plans to cut cybersecurity agency’s budget by $700 million

articles.description: Une sélection de formations destinée à celles et ceux souhaitant développer leurs compétences sur l'outil Canva.
